title: rubus hirtus var. celtidifolius
intro: Rubus hirtus var. celtidifolius, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its deciduous nature and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. celtidifolius include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used to create a herbal infusion or dried for later use.
